title: About Michael - Michael Wheeler
meta_description: Member of Parliament for Worsley & Eccles
headings: h3: “Serving the communities of Worsley & Eccles”
text_excerpt: About Michael - Michael Wheeler Skip to main content Michael Wheeler Member of Parliament for Worsley & Eccles Join Get Involved Donate About Michael How Can I Help You Personal Casework Contact Me My Work In Worsley & Eccles Constituency Surgery Information Upcoming Events Latest News In Parliament Committees APPGs Visiting Parliament More Search Search the website Search Menu About Michael Michael Wheeler MP “Serving the communities of Worsley & Eccles” Michael Wheeler has represented Worsley & Eccles as a Labour MP since 2024. His path into politics began locally, shaped by a belief in fairness, community, and the importance of giving people a voice. Before entering Parliament, Michael worked as a Political Officer for the USDAW trade union, supporting retail and distribution workers across the country. He went to Clarendon Road Primary School, Wentworth High School and Eccles Sixth From before studying at Lancaster University and has always been drawn to issues that affect everyday working lives. Michael was first elected to Salford City Council in a 2011 by-election for the Eccles ward. He served as a councillor for ten years, focusing on practical, local priorities and building strong relationships with residents. Since becoming an MP, Michael has continued that approach – listening, learning, and speaking up for the people he represents. Michael is proud to call this community home and is focused on making sure Worsley & Eccles gets the attention and investment it deserves.
